review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: Strained Work-Life Balance and Unyielding Culture. Limited Adaptability to Modern Work Styles.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Its a good organization for older generations and people who like traditional ways of working. It offers a great opportunity for those who enjoy dedicating their weekends and leaves to work, with competitive compensation packages and a picturesque campus. Office structure is beautiful.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: No work-life balance in this organization, and the adherence to traditional methods of operation creates a toxic and rigid environment. Specifically, within the EM department, I strongly advise against joining as the leadership is fast-paced and new employees often face bullying. The company's primary focus is on generating revenue, resulting in frequent burnouts among employees. Leaders work tirelessly, on all weekends and during their time off. This, in turn, has a domino effect on the entire team, leading to a significantly high attrition rate, which, in my opinion, is understandable given the circumstances. Overall, this organization may be suitable for individuals who are accustomed to the orthodox work culture prevalent in typical small-scale Indian organizations. However, if you are an ambitious, innovative employee who brings fresh perspectives, ideas, and a strong desire to make a meaningful impact by asking critical questions, I would advise you to look elsewhere as the organization seems incapable of adapting the modern ways of working. There are numerous other organizations that offer competitive compensation while providing opportunities to both contribute and receive value in a more fulfilling manner.

HR Support- Inspite of many constraints during this covid19 outbreak made my smooth virtual on-boarding by providing pre-reads about industry & project, necessary background material for project understanding, arranging the sessions & competitions and constant check-in progress of the project. 2. Guide & Mentor Support-Since the start of the project they have given me the holistic insight of the project to make my approach more relatable & in proper direction. They have provided constant support, guidance & mentor-ship throughout the entire journey. I was able to connect the dots and solve the challenges provided to me, only because of the brainstorming sessions we had & providing right direction to work upon. 3. Learning's- As I have assigned the live project on which already a team is working, I have got the holistic view of overall supply chain and able to learn out many new things like modelling, programming, statistics analysis and structured approach for enterprise solution etc.
4. Values & Culture- The culture of the organisation is very friendly & encouraging, they give you your space to operate, you can reach out to higher management directly, even higher management is very interested to listen your ideas & take them into considerations. review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
1) Good place to learn and absorb the technical and leadership skills. 
2) The program is well thought of with the defined goals and guidance directly from plant head, though the working may differ based on the mentors. 
3) The workings involves providing solutions and implementing the action plan to eliminate the ongoing problems of the plant based on productivity, safety, quality etc.
4) Will be very good to get an in hand experience in Operational excellence, TPM, production planning, people management, leadership skills, working in plant and will give a lot of opportunities to use chemical & mechanical expertise or rigorous modelling approach to resolve the issue. 
5) HR support is excellent.
6) If you are joining the deep domain training. I would advise you to join the Madhu Sundar' team at FTOPU-1. It is one of the best team you can find.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: 1) Working in Vizag plants involves a lot of travel time. Every day almost (3 hrs or more time) goes to travelling apart from the work which may disturb the work life balance.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
The program has been nicely framed to get maximum exposure in both the R&D and Manufacturing Stints. 

1. Trainees get to work on diverse yet correlated projects, enabling a holistic and broader understanding of industry. For example, one gets to work on the development of a product in R&D and work on the same product's commercial execution in plant. This helps to get a perspective from different teams and different situations.

2. Apart from our mentors and managers, the senior leaders, including CXOs are well within our reach.

3. The program will definitely fastrack our career to take leadership roles in future.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: People don't instill responsibilities early on in the stint. review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
Everyone is working multiple jobs (compared to working in big pharma where people work in silos). You have to manage your own time but if the opportunity is embraced, you can really learn a lot about the Pharma business because all of the teams are integrated for product development, filing and commercialization projects.

The work from home/time off is very flexible but varies depending on your supervisor.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
The culture of Quality is lacking. The quality team has to fight to keep quality and compliance in mind during the development process. This causes Quality to look like they are holding back time lines and costing projects more money but because Quality review and/or comments were not factored in, it does cause headaches and a push to avoid quality oversight by non-quality teams by skipping the review process. The non-quality teams do not understand the downstream impact. 
Onboarding is very unorganized. The team you will be working with doesn’t always know you are showing up. Training is not ready for you and depending on your team’s travel schedule, you will not be fully trained to perform your job duties for many months. 
The HR team in the US is strongly influenced by the team in India so they are much more likely to pull someone from India for a position then to promote or move a current associate in the US laterally who is just as qualified. 
I have recommended multiple people for jobs through the designated HR process and the  HR team lost/overlooked paperwork in one case, had assumed someone was over qualified and would not accept the salary in another case and in the 3rd case they had someone in mind before posting the position.
